claims.description: Verified unchanged from the prior landed pass (932333893, already on origin/main): the Navigation shell matrix's 'What's actually shipped today' build-profile table and its V2 column against apps/mobile/eas.json still hold — untouched by this rework, re-read against current source and confirmed accurate.

Verified unchanged from the prior landed pass and re-checked against current source this session: navigation-contract.ts is still 643 lines; STUDY_TABS still opens at line 153; V2_ROUTES is still at line 178; resolveCanEnter still opens at line 457. All four line numbers read directly against the file at this branch, not assumed from the prior pass.

Verified unchanged from the prior landed pass: the V2-CHROME-01/MENTOR-01/MENTOR-02/SUBJECTS-01/JOURNAL-01/SCOPE-01/SCOPE-02 rows' substantive Flow/Audience/Gating content (person-scope chips, person selection, scope-preserving tab changes, landing routes, back/exit, authorization boundaries) is untouched by this rework — only their Coverage cells changed, per AC-5.

Bounce-3 finding: the Status legend's own tagging test ("Which rows get one of these three tags") exempted a row from tagging whenever its content was judged shell-independent, so 233 of 288 retained rows — including ACCOUNT-07 through ACCOUNT-52 — carried no legacy-* tag at all, contradicting the AC's literal "every retained V0/V1 row is tagged" requirement. Fix: the exemption sentence and its section-scoped "applied exhaustively under this test" enumeration were removed and replaced with a universal rule — every retained row gets exactly one tag; shell-differential rows get the matching current/superseded/historical tag, and genuinely shell-independent rows still get legacy-current plus a short accurate note explaining why (renders pre-shell, reached via equivalent surfaces regardless of shell, orphaned/dormant in every flag state, etc.) rather than being exempted. All 227 previously-untagged rows across every section — Auth and Access, Profiles/Family/Consent/Account, Home/Navigation/Subject Setup, Learning/Chat/Library/Retention/Progress, Practice Hub (incl. Quiz/Dictation), Homework and Parent Experience, Billing and Monetization, Regression and System Flows (QA-*), and Cross-Cutting Behaviors (CC-*) — are now tagged; the only rows left untagged are the 6 new V2-only rows in "V2 Shell — Mentor, Subjects, Journal, Account" (V2-CHROME-01, V2-MENTOR-01/02, V2-SUBJECTS-01, V2-JOURNAL-01, V2-SCOPE-01), which have no V0/V1 predecessor to retain. Three cross-cutting rows (CC-19, CC-20, CC-22) were identified as genuinely shell-*specific* rather than shell-independent during the tagging pass and tagged legacy-current with a note naming the specific V0/V1-only mechanism (route-access gate machinery, Recaps/family-bridge return targets, the V0-only app-help map) instead of the generic shell-independent note. All 288 row IDs now carry exactly one legacy-* tag (verified: `grep -c` for the three tag strings against the full row-ID list yields 288, matching `extractDefinedRowIds`' count minus the 6 V2-only rows plus their own non-applicability). Scope note for the reviewer: whether the two audience-agnostic sections (QA-*, CC-*) should carry this V0/V1-legacy tag at all was a judgment call, not explicit in the reviewer's cited examples (which were all ACCOUNT-*) — resolved in favor of the non-narrowing reading of the AC's literal "every retained V0/V1 row" and the ruling's "every other pre-V2 row", since excluding them would itself be an AC-narrowing decision reserved for PM ruling, not this rework.

A new 'Manifest Reconciliation (AC-5)' section reconciles all 187 distinct manifest files cited across the doc's 449 backtick citations, per the cite-rot guard's own count (not a subset of batch-touched rows), with a per-manifest path-exists / runs-under-CI-trigger / proves-flow table. The CI-trigger facts are mechanical: each Maestro flow's real tags: frontmatter is read against ci-maestro-plan.mjs's suite-selection logic (nightly cron vs PR gate vs the v2 workflow_dispatch suite vs untriggered tags like weekly/blocked/manual/devclient), and each Playwright spec against its playwright.config.ts project testMatch (web smoke vs full suite vs opt-in-only projects). 'Proves flow' starts from each citing row's own existing coverage annotation and is downgraded where this pass found the mechanical fact narrows or contradicts it. Two concrete drift findings corrected: (1) the V2 Shell section's intro falsely claimed no dedicated Maestro/Playwright flow targets the 3-tab shell and mis-cited the scope-switching journey as a nonexistent 'V2-05' — three real, already-landed native Maestro flows (v2-shell-navigation.yaml, v2-supporter-scope-journey.yaml, v2-subject-create-round-trip.yaml, wired to the v2 workflow_dispatch suite) are now cited against the correct V2-CHROME-01/MENTOR-01/MENTOR-02/SUBJECTS-01/JOURNAL-01/SCOPE-01 rows; (2) BILLING-06's 'PARKED / out-of-manifest' claim for billing/child-paywall.yaml is stale — the file's live YAML tags: frontmatter carries 'nightly' (so the tag-based nightly-suite selection in ci-maestro-plan.mjs does select it, contradicting the file's own header comment), and the same file's own 'KNOWN BREAK' comment documents that it currently times out post-ACCOUNT-04 rather than passing; both are marked in the row and the table, not silently fixed (test-flow/CI-config changes are out of this docs-only item's scope, flagged as a Gap instead).
Verified unchanged from the prior landed pass: the 'What's actually shipped today' table and its no-universal-default wording are untouched by this rework.

scripts/check-flow-inventory-cite-rot.ts gains three mechanical checks beyond citation existence, addressing the reviewer's finding that the guard only caught cite-rot, not flag/shell-state drift: (1) row-ID cross-links — every bare ID-shaped token referenced from another row's prose must resolve to a real defined row or an explicitly-removed one, with a dedicated rule for the exact bug class the reviewer found (a bare 'V2-NN' token, since real V2-shell rows are always three-part IDs); (2) flag tokens — every MODE_NAV_V*_ENABLED / EXPO_PUBLIC_ENABLE_MODE_NAV* token cited must be a real symbol in feature-flags.ts; (3) legacy tags — every inline **legacy-xxx** tag must be one of the three the Status legend defines. All three are cite-rot-style mechanical checks (regex + set-membership against real source), not a new test harness, matching the AC's own refinement note. The guard caught the exact V2-05 bug before it was fixed (verified by running the guard against the doc pre-fix) and is clean post-fix. Co-located test suite covers each new check's pass/fail paths plus the false-positive guard (BUG-NNN/WI-NNN tokens are correctly ignored). Wired into docs-checks.yml's existing flow-inventory-cite-rot job (unchanged — same script, same job).

Refinement notes still satisfied: no product code changed this rework either (docs/flows/mobile-app-flow-inventory.md plus the one existing tooling script, extended). e2e-web smoke is still never treated as full-layout-claim evidence; the new AC-5 table makes the smoke/full/opt-in distinction explicit per manifest rather than leaving it implicit. AC-7's guard additions stayed mechanical (regex/set-membership against real source), not a new test harness.
